item.title: HSBC Conducted ‘Thorough’ Review of Fraud Provision, Chair Says
item.description: HSBC Holdings Plc has carried out a “thorough review” of a $400 million fraud-related provision in its latest earnings and is updating its risk appetite, according to Chairman Brendan Nelson.

item.title: Analysis of US Jobs Report for April
item.description: Jobseekers speak with recruiters during a job fair in Fletcher, North Carolina. Photographer: Allison Joyce/Bloomberg

item.title: AI Startup Monk Offers a Year’s Rent for One Good Recruiting Tip
item.description: The company is making a creative pitch to find engineers in a highly competitive market

item.title: Salmon Farms on Land Take Aim at a $19 Billion Industry
item.description: They could upend the fishing sector—but only if companies can bring the costs down first.

item.title: New Oil Shock Prompts Asian Governments to Intervene in Markets
item.description: Governments across Asia are intervening into fossil fuel markets to try and smooth over disruptions.

item.title: Box Makers Struggle to Pass on War Costs as Demand Stays Weak
item.description: Packaging producers can’t catch a break as the conflict in the Middle East deals the latest blow to the industry’s protracted downturn.

item.title: Black Eyed Peas Co-Founder will.i.am Is Also an AI Evangelist
item.description: Black Eyed Peas co-founder will.i.am has a message for young people worried that artificial intelligence will make it hard for them to get jobs: embrace the technology, he says, and make it work for you.

item.title: Uber-Backed Electric Scooter Rental Firm Lime Files for IPO
item.description: Electric bike and scooter rental startup Lime filed for an initial public offering, showing growing revenue and an expanding user base.

item.title: Treasuries Gain Ahead of US Payrolls With Jobs Growth Expected
item.description: Treasuries gained ahead of US employment data that looks set to cement the Federal Reserve’s cautious approach to changing policy.

item.title: DoubleLine’s Jeffrey Gundlach Is Preparing for an Extreme Debt Scenario
item.description: DoubleLine Capital’s Jeffrey Gundlach thinks the US government might restructure its debt.

item.title: Trump Wants to Make H-1B Workers More Expensive for US Employers
item.description: An entry‑level software engineer in San Francisco would need to be paid $162,000 a year to qualify for an H‑1B visa under a Trump administration proposal — almost 30% more than today. In Dallas, the minimum would jump by a similar rate to $113,000 and in New York to $132,000.

item.title: Intesa Sanpaolo Beats on Profit as Trading Revenue Surges
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA‘s first-quarter results came in better than expected on the back of a jump in trading revenue.

item.title: Japan Likely Refrained From Currency Intervention After Holidays
item.description: Japanese authorities likely refrained from intervening in the currency market on Thursday, the first business day of this week, after multiple apparent actions during the long holiday weekend, according to a Bloomberg analysis of central bank accounts.

item.title: Coinbase Resumes Trading After Seven-Hour Exchange Outage
item.description: Crypto exchange Coinbase Global Inc. said trading services had resumed after a significant outage left customers unable to transact on its platform for almost seven hours on Friday, rounding off a difficult week at the company.

item.title: London Vote Offers Snapshot of Fractured and Fractious UK
item.description: The map of English councils was lighting up in a murky gray on Friday morning, as more and more town halls returned a divided composition that spells future budget paralysis.

item.title: Pakistan Plans Debut Panda Bond Sale of Up to 1.75 Billion Yuan
item.description: Pakistan is looking to raise yuan-denominated bonds in China’s onshore market, in what would mark its debut panda bond issuance, with a size of as much as 1.75 billion yuan ($257 million).

item.title: Intel CEO Who Won Over Trump and Musk Now Needs a Breakthrough
item.description: After Lip-Bu Tan became chief executive officer of Intel Corp. in March of last year, the struggling company’s shares went nowhere for seven months while the chipmaker was getting trounced in the market for artificial intelligence.

item.title: UK Bonds Rally as Starmer Says He’ll Stay as Prime Minister
item.description: UK government bonds rallied as Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he would stay as the Labour leader despite the party’s election losses.

item.title: BofA Didn’t Have Block Trade Mandate at Time of Segantii Calls
item.description: Bank of America Corp. didn’t have a mandate to work on a block trade in Esprit Holdings Ltd. when it had phone calls with Segantii Capital Management to discuss the deal, a prosecution witness said during the hedge fund’s trial for insider trading.

item.title: Gold Fields’ Spat With Ghana Contractor Heads for Arbitration
item.description: A $740 million dispute between Gold Fields Ltd. and a mining contractor in Ghana will be fought out in arbitration.

item.title: FSD Africa to Raise $400 Million SME Financing for Five Nations
item.description: FSD Africa, an agency backed by the UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office, is establishing funds to raise as much as $400 million of capital for small businesses in five nations.

item.title: BofA’s Hartnett Says US Stock Gains Have Rarely Been This Strong
item.description: US stocks and gold are hurtling toward a fourth year of double-digit gains, rallies so exceptional that there have been few such episodes in history, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

item.title: Oil’s Price Surge Spurs Nigeria’s Flip From Discount to Darling
item.description: Nigerian assets are rallying across stocks, bonds and the currency as investor confidence builds in President Bola Tinubu’s economic agenda.

item.title: Rheinmetall Loses Bull in JPMorgan After Slew of Results Misses
item.description: Rheinmetall AG’s position as the standout European defense stock is looking increasingly vulnerable after a series of disappointing results led a long-time bull to downgrade the shares.

item.title: Rheinmetall CEO Sees High Mideast Demand for Air Defense Systems
item.description: Rheinmetall AG sees growing demand for its air defense systems in the Middle East amid the Iran conflict and hopes to nearly double the number in use there by the end of 2027, the company’s chief executive officer said in an interview.

item.title: Shares of India’s Top Lender Slump on Interest-Income Miss
item.description: State Bank of India’s shares tumbled the most in three months after the nation’s top lender by assets reported weaker-than-expected net interest income.

item.title: Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic India Price Cut Boosts Obesity Drug Sales
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S saw a 40% surge in sales of its diabetes and weight-loss drugs in India last month, after steep price cuts boosted demand for its branded treatments even as generic GLP-1 therapies flood the market.

item.title: Bulgaria Lawmakers Approve Radev’s Cabinet After Landslide Win
item.description: Bulgaria’s parliament voted in the nation’s first single-party government with a majority this century after Rumen Radev’s landslide victory, ushering in another European Union leader critical of the bloc’s mainstream.

item.title: South East Water CEO Quits After Pressure From UK Lawmakers
item.description: The embattled chief executive officer of the UK’s South East Water resigned after a campaign by lawmakers and customers in the wake of multiple outages across Kent that affected tens of thousands of people.

item.title: M&G’s Fedeli Shifts to Cheaper Stocks After Tech’s Rapid Rally
item.description: M&G Investment Management is taking profit in technology stocks that have seen rapid gains and is instead seeking undervalued companies with strong fundamentals given volatile markets, said executive Fabiana Fedeli.
